adjudication_notes: Iqbal must review each text and the proposed labels. Promote labels to gold by setting labeler_role='gold' and adjudicator='Iqbal'. Add/remove labels as needed. Negative items (expected_signals=[]) should also be reviewed — confirm extractor truly should produce nothing.

signal_taxonomy.price_mention: Text references price, cost, fees, discount, currency, affordability
signal_taxonomy.purchase_trigger: Text describes a purchase decision, choice, or selection event
signal_taxonomy.rejection: Text describes refusing, avoiding, returning, or skipping a product
signal_taxonomy.switching: Text describes moving from one product/brand to another
signal_taxonomy.trust_citation: Text cites an external authority (doctor, expert, friend, review)

items.text: I bought the Nikon at half the price after my friend said it was the best.
The fee was too expensive, so I cancelled the subscription.
I switched from Crest to Sensodyne after my dermatologist recommended it.
I refused to buy it because the reviews were bad.
Picked up a new laptop yesterday on a steep discount.
The doctor told me Lipitor was safer, so I changed to it.
I went with the cheaper option since I'm on a budget.
I tried the new flavor but returned it the next day.
My nutritionist said the protein powder was certified organic.
Won't buy anything from that brand again after the last fiasco.
The weather was nice and we walked to the park.
My morning routine includes coffee and reading the newspaper.
I'm thinking about visiting my parents next month.
The conference was insightful but the venue was poorly chosen.
Costly mistake — should have read the contract more carefully.
items.notes: price keyword present (costly) but no purchase action; review whether 'costly mistake' should fire price_mention

items.text: Replaced my old phone with the latest model.
According to Consumer Reports, this brand of dishwasher is the most reliable.
The free trial convinced me to subscribe.
Skipped the latest iPhone because it wasn't worth the upgrade.
Decided to buy after the demo showed real time savings.
I'm no longer using Slack — moved the team to Notion.
Affordable, reliable, and the customer service is great.
items.notes: affordable triggers price; review if 'great service' should add anything

items.text: The expert on the panel praised its build quality.
My friend said the new espresso machine is incredible — I ordered one immediately.
The price didn't justify the marginal improvement, so I passed on it.
I wanted to try the new Tesla but the cost was prohibitive.
items.notes: price implied; 'tried' not used in past-tense purchase sense — review

items.text: Trusted the certification and bought the organic version.
items.notes: review: 'organic' alone shouldn't fire price; 'bought' fires purchase; 'certified/trusted' fires trust

items.text: Reading is my main hobby on weekends.
The garden looks beautiful this season.
Refused the upgrade offer because I'd just bought the previous version.
items.notes: rejection + past purchase reference; review whether 'just bought' should count
